# PDTF 2.0 — Property Data Trust Framework

> The Property Data Trust Framework (PDTF) 2.0 is an open specification for representing, signing, and verifying property transaction data using W3C Verifiable Credentials, Decentralised Identifiers (DIDs), and a federated Trusted Issuer Registry.

## Overview

PDTF 2.0 replaces the OpenID Connect verified claims model with W3C Verifiable Credentials. It decomposes the monolithic property transaction schema into nine entity types — Transaction, Property, Title, Person, Organisation, Ownership, Representation, DelegatedConsent, and Offer — each independently identifiable and independently verifiable.

Key features:
- Entity graph: 9 entity types with stable identifiers (DIDs, URNs)
- W3C Verifiable Credentials Data Model v2.0 with eddsa-jcs-2022 signatures
- Decentralised Identifiers: did:key (persons), did:web (organisations, transactions)
- Trusted Issuer Registry: federated trust model for credential issuance
- Bitstring Status List: credential revocation
- Backward compatibility: state assembly functions for v3 format

## Specifications

- Architecture Overview: https://propdata.org.uk/specs/00-architecture-overview/
- Entity Graph & Schema: https://propdata.org.uk/specs/01-entity-graph/
- VC Data Model: https://propdata.org.uk/specs/02-vc-data-model/
- DID Methods & Identifiers: https://propdata.org.uk/specs/03-did-methods/
- Trusted Issuer Registry: https://propdata.org.uk/specs/04-trusted-issuer-registry/
- Key Management: https://propdata.org.uk/specs/06-key-management/
- State Assembly: https://propdata.org.uk/specs/07-state-assembly/
- Reference Implementations: https://propdata.org.uk/specs/13-reference-implementations/
- Credential Revocation: https://propdata.org.uk/specs/14-credential-revocation/

## Documentation

- Architecture Overview: https://propdata.org.uk/architecture/overview/
- Entity Graph: https://propdata.org.uk/architecture/entities/
- Developer Quickstart: https://propdata.org.uk/docs/quickstart/

## Key Concepts

### Entity Types
- Transaction (did:web) — sale metadata, status, milestones
- Property (urn:pdtf:uprn:{uprn}) — physical property data, the "logbook"
- Title (urn:pdtf:titleNumber:{number}) — legal title from HM Land Registry
- Person (did:key) — natural person, role-free
- Organisation (did:web) — law firm, estate agency, lender
- Ownership — links Person to Title, revocable
- Representation — delegates authority to Organisation, revocable
- DelegatedConsent — authorised data access
- Offer — links buyer to Transaction

### Trust Model
Three-phase evolution:
1. Trusted proxies (current) — small number of organisations re-issue data as VCs
2. Independent adapters — third parties build and host their own adapters
3. Primary source issuers — data sources issue VCs directly

## Organisation

- Publisher: Property Data Standards Company
- GitHub: https://github.com/property-data-standards-co
- Website: https://propdata.org.uk
